title: The most memorable motif from Evangelion
display_title: The most memorable motif from Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: The most memorable motif from Evangelion             Quote Post by BeoX2 » Tue Aug 24, 2010 1:55 am What is the most memorable motif for you from the series? My personal one is the cicadas. I literally remember more of that than the dialogue. I'm not quite sure what it symbolizes, but I'll never think or hear cicadas the same again.

[]:             Quote Post by Darkman.exe213 » Tue Aug 24, 2010 2:18 am The cross-shaped explosions. It doesn't make every cross I encounter remind me of Evangelion, but when I was first watching the series, the crosses definitely stood out in my mind, and made me think a bit.

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Tue Aug 24, 2010 3:43 am Evangelion Unit 01´s color scheme. Especially when it glows and stuff.

[]:             Quote Post by Yazakel » Tue Aug 24, 2010 8:12 am Either has to be the angel's red cores or the concentric octagon ATF shield. Similar objects always link back to NGE in my mind, but yeah i agree with the cruciform explosions. (One of the first images that stuck in my head from the series) Oh yeah, also see my sig

[]:             Quote Post by WonderBug » Tue Aug 24, 2010 10:10 am Darn you, I was going to say "cicadas!" I feel the same way you do, it's basically what I hear in my mind when I think of NGE.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Tue Aug 24, 2010 10:12 am Hands.

[]:             Quote Post by soul.assassin » Tue Aug 24, 2010 10:14 am Trinities. A great deal of them. And strangulation/throttling (more frequently in the manga).

[]:             Quote Post by planet news » Tue Aug 24, 2010 12:27 pm An unfamiliar ceiling. It frequently applies to real life.

[]:             Quote Post by Bomby von Bombsville » Tue Aug 24, 2010 12:54 pm My hand. What is it used for? To grip with confidence and take control of shit!

[]:             Quote Post by Hawq » Tue Aug 24, 2010 1:50 pm To jerk it to coma girls? at least they haven't revisited that scene.... yet

[]:             Quote Post by LeoXiao » Tue Aug 24, 2010 3:51 pm The overabundance of infrastructure/military objects really struck a chord with me; it was great to see all these constructs of man being built and destroyed by the Angels, and to see them being used like toys by NERV in the ultimate battle for man's survival. There are lots of examples; some better ones include Rei's usage of the space shuttle as a shield, the part where Asuka pwns the JSSDF troops with the destroyer, or the episode 8 naval battle.

[]:             Quote Post by berto » Tue Aug 24, 2010 6:49 pm The stage.

[]:             Quote Post by SpearofLonginus » Tue Aug 24, 2010 6:53 pm WonderBug wrote: Darn you, I was going to say "cicadas!" I feel the same way you do, it's basically what I hear in my mind when I think of NGE.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Tue Aug 24, 2010 7:03 pm Hands, because I contemplate them.

[]:             Quote Post by shinji_ryoji_89 » Tue Aug 24, 2010 7:57 pm The "Exit" signs. Amazing how such a simple, everyday thing can seem so meaningful in a work of art like Evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Aug 24, 2010 9:20 pm I would say hands is the most brilliantly executed motif, but I had to choose the most memorable--the one that stuck out in my mind from my first viewing--I would also have to say the Religious iconography like the cross.

[]:             Quote Post by BeoX2 » Tue Aug 24, 2010 9:51 pm Okay, so can someone tell me what they mean when they say "hands?"

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Aug 24, 2010 10:01 pm BeoX2 wrote: Okay, so can someone tell me what they mean when they say "hands?"

[]:             Quote Post by BeoX2 » Tue Aug 24, 2010 10:11 pm Does that have anything to do with when in EoE Rei, during Komm Susser Tod, asks "Then what is your hand for?"

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Aug 24, 2010 10:24 pm ^ Yep, you even see an image of Shinji clenching/unclenching his hand when she says it.

I literally remember more of that than the dialogue. I'm not quite sure what it symbolizes, but I'll never think or hear cicadas the same again.
